parsed.answer: Absolute pitch (perfect pitch) is rare, affecting approximately 1 in 1,000 to 1 in 10,000 people in the general population. Prevalence is significantly higher among trained musicians (estimates range from 1 in 100 to 1 in 10 among music students). The exact prevalence varies by study methodology, population sampled, and how strictly absolute pitch is defined. Globally, this would translate to roughly 8-80 million people with some degree of absolute pitch ability, depending on the threshold used.
